preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia at 41%, while Cape Verde has a 28% chance to win. The underdog's probability trails the favorite by 13 percentage points, establishing Saudi Arabia as the clear preferred outcome under the statistical forecast.

The draw carries substantial weight at 31%, making this a genuinely three-way contest. The win probabilities are spread across all outcomes without dominance by either team, suggesting a competitive match where no result would constitute a major upset. A 1–1 or 0–0 finish ranks as plausible as an outright Saudi Arabia victory.

The model and sportsbooks diverge notably on the favorite. Bookmakers and Polymarket both price Cape Verde's win probability around 41%, roughly 12 percentage points higher than the model's assessment. Meanwhile, both market sources place Saudi Arabia significantly lower—near 32%—compared to the model's 41%. The two sides are nearly aligned on draw probability. This dashboard presents both forecasts without claiming superiority; the divergence reflects genuine disagreement about the match's likely outcome.
